chunk_summary: This section discusses consent, guardian verification, and media handling in pediatric tele-nutrition, emphasizing safeguarding, documentation, and ethical media use in Indian tele-consults.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of verifying guardian identity and consent in pediatric tele-nutrition.
Learn how to document caregiver relationships, media exchange, and consultation details to mitigate medico-legal risks.
Recognize safeguarding protocols for child safety concerns during tele-consults.
Implement ethical media handling practices, including consent for photos/videos and file management.
key_facts: Consent for minors must come from a verified parent, adult sibling, or legally appointed guardian.
Tele-notes should document the caregiver’s identity, relationship to the child, and who was present during the consult.
Media handling requires explicit documented consent for recording, screenshots, and storage of photos/videos.
Safeguarding protocols must be followed if child safety concerns (e.g., neglect, abuse) are suspected.
Fee transparency is part of consent, including documentation of consultation fees and follow-up terms.
reference_quote: ‘If child safety concerns are suspected (neglect, abuse, caregiver incapacity), escalate according to local safeguarding protocols rather than continuing routine nutrition counseling.’ (Telemedicine Practice Guidelines, India)
document_key_topics: Consent and guardian verification in pediatric tele-nutrition
